mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held near the zero lower bound, the discount rate channel dominates and raises the present value of future S&P 500 cash flows, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods of falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, reduces equity risk appetite, and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0% and is up at least 0.25 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS marks labor-market deterioration, which weakens earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration signal does not outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns occur in more than half of high-deterioration months.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and revenue growth expectations, supporting S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon through improved near-term sales revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS grows above its 3-month moving average by at least 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if breakouts in RSAFS are followed by negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from random after transaction-cost adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in INDPRO and HOUST signals broad real-sector production and construction strength, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint industrial-housing signal fails to deliver positive 60-day forward returns on average, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 confirms aggregate demand expansion and improves forward corporate revenue expectations, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its prior quarter and annualized growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with positive GDPC1 growth do not show above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE anchors household income risk and reduces recession probability, which supports equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 6.0% and not rising by more than 0.2 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment regimes do not outperform the unconditional 60-day SPX return distribution, or if the effect disappears once the unemployment trend is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates turn more negative and liquidity conditions stay easy, which supports a short-horizon equity rally over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-zero-rate configuration does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances.
